title: rubus moluccanus var. glabriusculus
intro: Rubus moluccanus var. glabriusculus, also known as the Himalayan raspberry or mountain raspberry, is a variant of the Rubus moluccanus plant that belongs to the Rosaceae family. It is a type of woody vine or shrub that has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as treating digestive issues and other ailments. Rubus moluccanus var. glabriusculus can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also used in traditional folk medicine to make salves and ointments for topical application.
